#### 9.7.14.2. [Matrix Data-types](#warp-level-matrix-data-types)

The matrix multiply and accumulate operation is supported separately on integer, floating-point,
sub-byte integer and single bit data-types. All operands must contain the same basic type kind,
i.e., integer or floating-point.

For floating-point matrix multiply and accumulate operation, different matrix operands may have
different precision, as described later.

| Data-type | Multiplicands (A or B) | Accumulators (C or D) |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Integer | `.u8`, `.s8` | `.s32` |
| Floating Point | `.f16` | `.f16`, `.f32` |
| Alternate floating Point | `.bf16` | `.f32` |
| Alternate floating Point | `.tf32` | `.f32` |
| Alternate floating Point | `.e4m3` or `.e5m2` or `.e3m2` or `.e2m3` or `.e2m1` | `.f16`, `.f32` |
| Alternate floating Point with scale | `.e4m3` or `.e5m2` or `.e3m2` or `.e2m3` or `.e2m1` X (Scale) `.ue8m0` | `.f32` |
| Alternate floating Point with scale | `.e2m1` X (Scale) `.ue8m0` or `.ue4m3` | `.f32` |
| Floating Point | `.f64` | `.f64` |
| Sub-byte integer | both `.u4` or both `.s4` | `.s32` |
| Single-bit integer | `.b1` | `.s32` |

#### 9.7.14.3. [Block Scaling for `mma.sync`](#warp-level-block-scaling)

The `mma` instruction with the following `.kind` qualifier:

* `.kind::mxf8f6f4`
* `.kind::mxf4`
* `.kind::mxf4nvf4`

perform matrix multiplication with block scaling. This operation has the following form:
`D = (A * scale_A) * (B * scale_B) + C`.

For a `scale_A` matrix of shape *M x SFA_N*, each row of matrix `A` is divided into
*SFA_N* number of chunks and each chunk of a row is multiplied with the corresponding
element (henceforth referred as *SF_A*) from the same row of `scale_A`.

Similarly, for a `scale_B` matrix of shape *SFB_M x N*, each column of matrix `B` is
divided into the *SFB_M* number of chunks and each chunk of a column is multiplied with
the corresponding element (henceforth referred as *SF_B*) from the same column of `scale_B`.

[Figure 42](#mma-block-scaling) shows an example of `mma` with block scaling of `scale_vec::2X`.





[Figure: mma-block-scaling]
CONCEPT BEING ILLUSTRATED:

This figure illustrates the matrix operand layout for the mma (matrix multiply-accumulate) instruction with block scaling using the scale_vec::2X modifier in NVIDIA PTX ISA. It shows how the input matrices A and B, their corresponding scale matrices (scale-A and scale-B), and the output/accumulator matrix C/D are organized and dimensioned relative to each other.

OVERALL LAYOUT:

The figure contains five distinct colored rectangular blocks arranged to suggest a matrix multiplication operation: D = A * B + C (or equivalently the output D replaces C). The arrangement follows the standard matrix multiplication layout where Matrix A is on the lower-left, Matrix B is on the upper-right, and Matrix C/D (accumulator/output) is on the lower-right. Scale matrices are positioned adjacent to their corresponding data matrices.

INDIVIDUAL COMPONENTS:

1. MATRIX A (green rectangle, lower-left):
   - Color: Green
   - Dimensions labeled: K (horizontal, width) and M (vertical, height)
   - The K dimension is indicated by a double-headed arrow spanning the full width of Matrix A, positioned above it
   - The M dimension is indicated by a double-headed arrow on the left side, spanning the full height
   - Matrix A is divided vertically into two halves by a vertical line through the middle (indicating the 2X block scaling subdivision along K)
   - Label: "Matrix A" appears below the rectangle

2. SCALE-A MATRIX (blue rectangle, immediately to the right of Matrix A):
   - Color: Blue
   - This is a narrow vertical rectangle positioned directly adjacent to the right edge of Matrix A
   - It has the same height (M dimension) as Matrix A
   - It appears to be divided into two narrow vertical columns (two thin blue strips side by side), corresponding to the 2X scaling (one scale value per half of K)
   - Label: "scale-A matrix" appears below the rectangle

3. MATRIX B (green rectangle, upper-right):
   - Color: Green
   - Dimensions labeled: N (horizontal, width) and K (vertical, height)
   - The N dimension is indicated by a double-headed arrow spanning the full width, positioned above Matrix B
   - The K dimension is indicated by a double-headed arrow on the left side of Matrix B, spanning the full height
   - Matrix B is divided horizontally into two halves by a horizontal line through the middle (indicating the 2X block scaling subdivision along K)
   - Label: "Matrix B" appears to the right of the rectangle

4. SCALE-B MATRIX (blue rectangle, below Matrix B):
   - Color: Blue
   - This is a wide, short horizontal rectangle positioned directly below Matrix B
   - It has the same width (N dimension) as Matrix B
   - It appears to consist of two thin horizontal rows stacked (two thin blue strips), corresponding to the 2X scaling (one scale value per half of K)
   - Label: "scale-B matrix" appears to the right of the rectangle

5. MATRIX C/D (yellow/orange rectangle, lower-right):
   - Color: Yellow/Orange
   - This represents both the input accumulator matrix C and the output matrix D
   - Dimensions correspond to M (height) x N (width), consistent with the result of an M x K times K x N multiplication
   - Label: "Matrix C/D" appears inside the rectangle
   - No internal subdivisions

DIMENSIONAL RELATIONSHIPS:

- Matrix A is M rows by K columns
- Matrix B is K rows by N columns
- Matrix C/D is M rows by N columns (the result)
- The K dimension is shared between Matrix A (width) and Matrix B (height), as expected in matrix multiplication
- The scale_vec::2X modifier means K is divided into 2 equal blocks, hence:
  - Matrix A is split into 2 vertical halves along K
  - Matrix B is split into 2 horizontal halves along K
  - scale-A matrix has 2 columns (one scale factor per K-block, per row of A), dimensions M x 2
  - scale-B matrix has 2 rows (one scale factor per K-block, per column of B), dimensions 2 x N

BLOCK SCALING INTERPRETATION:

The 2X in scale_vec::2X indicates that there are 2 scale vectors applied along the K dimension. Each half of K in Matrix A has a corresponding column in the scale-A matrix, and each half of K in Matrix B has a corresponding row in the scale-B matrix. This block scaling allows different scaling factors to be applied to different segments of the K dimension during the matrix multiply-accumulate operation.

DATA FLOW:

The computation proceeds as: D[m,n] = sum over k of (scale_A[m, k_block] * A[m,k] * scale_B[k_block, n] * B[k,n]) + C[m,n], where k_block indexes which of the 2 K-dimension blocks the element belongs to.

CONCEPT BEING ILLUSTRATED:

This figure illustrates the matrix operand layout for the mma (matrix multiply-accumulate) instruction with block scaling using the scale_vec::2X modifier in NVIDIA PTX ISA. It shows how the input matrices A and B, their corresponding scale matrices (scale-A and scale-B), and the output/accumulator matrix C/D are organized and dimensioned relative to each other.

OVERALL LAYOUT:

The figure contains five distinct colored rectangular blocks arranged to suggest a matrix multiplication operation: D = A * B + C (or equivalently the output D replaces C). The arrangement follows the standard matrix multiplication layout where Matrix A is on the lower-left, Matrix B is on the upper-right, and Matrix C/D (accumulator/output) is on the lower-right. Scale matrices are positioned adjacent to their corresponding data matrices.

INDIVIDUAL COMPONENTS:

1. MATRIX A (green rectangle, lower-left):
   - Color: Green
   - Dimensions labeled: K (horizontal, width) and M (vertical, height)
   - The K dimension is indicated by a double-headed arrow spanning the full width of Matrix A, positioned above it
   - The M dimension is indicated by a double-headed arrow on the left side, spanning the full height
   - Matrix A is divided vertically into two halves by a vertical line through the middle (indicating the 2X block scaling subdivision along K)
   - Label: "Matrix A" appears below the rectangle

2. SCALE-A MATRIX (blue rectangle, immediately to the right of Matrix A):
   - Color: Blue
   - This is a narrow vertical rectangle positioned directly adjacent to the right edge of Matrix A
   - It has the same height (M dimension) as Matrix A
   - It appears to be divided into two narrow vertical columns (two thin blue strips side by side), corresponding to the 2X scaling (one scale value per half of K)
   - Label: "scale-A matrix" appears below the rectangle

3. MATRIX B (green rectangle, upper-right):
   - Color: Green
   - Dimensions labeled: N (horizontal, width) and K (vertical, height)
   - The N dimension is indicated by a double-headed arrow spanning the full width, positioned above Matrix B
   - The K dimension is indicated by a double-headed arrow on the left side of Matrix B, spanning the full height
   - Matrix B is divided horizontally into two halves by a horizontal line through the middle (indicating the 2X block scaling subdivision along K)
   - Label: "Matrix B" appears to the right of the rectangle

4. SCALE-B MATRIX (blue rectangle, below Matrix B):
   - Color: Blue
   - This is a wide, short horizontal rectangle positioned directly below Matrix B
   - It has the same width (N dimension) as Matrix B
   - It appears to consist of two thin horizontal rows stacked (two thin blue strips), corresponding to the 2X scaling (one scale value per half of K)
   - Label: "scale-B matrix" appears to the right of the rectangle

5. MATRIX C/D (yellow/orange rectangle, lower-right):
   - Color: Yellow/Orange
   - This represents both the input accumulator matrix C and the output matrix D
   - Dimensions correspond to M (height) x N (width), consistent with the result of an M x K times K x N multiplication
   - Label: "Matrix C/D" appears inside the rectangle
   - No internal subdivisions

DIMENSIONAL RELATIONSHIPS:

- Matrix A is M rows by K columns
- Matrix B is K rows by N columns
- Matrix C/D is M rows by N columns (the result)
- The K dimension is shared between Matrix A (width) and Matrix B (height), as expected in matrix multiplication
- The scale_vec::2X modifier means K is divided into 2 equal blocks, hence:
  - Matrix A is split into 2 vertical halves along K
  - Matrix B is split into 2 horizontal halves along K
  - scale-A matrix has 2 columns (one scale factor per K-block, per row of A), dimensions M x 2
  - scale-B matrix has 2 rows (one scale factor per K-block, per column of B), dimensions 2 x N

BLOCK SCALING INTERPRETATION:

The 2X in scale_vec::2X indicates that there are 2 scale vectors applied along the K dimension. Each half of K in Matrix A has a corresponding column in the scale-A matrix, and each half of K in Matrix B has a corresponding row in the scale-B matrix. This block scaling allows different scaling factors to be applied to different segments of the K dimension during the matrix multiply-accumulate operation.

DATA FLOW:

The computation proceeds as: D[m,n] = sum over k of (scale_A[m, k_block] * A[m,k] * scale_B[k_block, n] * B[k,n]) + C[m,n], where k_block indexes which of the 2 K-dimension blocks the element belongs to.


Figure 42 `mma` with block scaling of `.scale_vec::2X`

The shapes for `scale_A` and `scale_B` matrices depend upon the qualifier `.scale_vec_size`
as shown in [Table 35](#mma-scale-vec-matrix-shape).

Table 35 Shapes for scale matrices depending upon `.scale_vec_size` qualifier

| .scale_vec_size | Shape of scale_A | Shape of scale_B |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `.scale_vec::1X` | M x 1 | 1 x N |
| `.scale_vec::2X` | M x 2 | 2 x N |
| `.scale_vec::4X` | M x 4 | 4 x N |

The valid combination of the exact element types and the `.scale_vec_size` are listed in
[Table 36](#mma-scaling-kind-type-valid-combination).

Table 36 Valid combinations of `.scale_vec_size` and `.kind` qualifier

| .kind::\* | Element Data Type .atype and .btype | Scale Data Type .stype | .scale_vec_size |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `.kind::mxf8f6f4` | `.e4m3`, `.e5m2` `.e3m2`, `.e2m3` `.e2m1` | `.ue8m0` | `.scale_vec::1X` |
| `.kind::mxf4` | `.e2m1` | `.ue8m0` | `.scale_vec::2X` |
| `.kind::mxf4nvf4` | `.e2m1` | `.ue8m0` | `.scale_vec::2X`, `.scale_vec::4X` |
| `.e2m1` | `.ue4m3` | `.scale_vec::4X` |

The `scale-a-data` and `scale-b-data` argument provides metadata for `scale_A` and
`scale_B` matrices respectively. The tuple `{byte-id-a, thread-id-a}` and
`{byte-id-b, thread-id-b}` provides the selector information to choose elements
*SF_A* and *SF_B* from corresponding metadata arguments `scale-a-data` and
`scale-b-data`.
The tuple `{byte-id-a, thread-id-a}` allows to select the scale matrix element *SF_A*
from `scale-a-data`. Similarly, the tuple `{byte-id-b, thread-id-b}` allows to select
the scale matrix element *SF_B* from `scale-b-data`.
